items.reason: DEC-001 through DEC-020 all populate id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to with concrete artifact references in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`. The 20 areas listed in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` each map to a decision with all six fields populated.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03), each with description, rationale, blocker, owner, and target date. Both target dates are in the future relative to current iteration timestamps (~2026-04-27).

Each DEC consequences section names what is removed with concrete artifact references — e.g., DEC-007 removes per-table SIS/LTI/email columns naming `public.source_identifiers`; DEC-010 removes duplicated tenant `where tenant_id=` guards naming PostgREST and `demo/server.js`; DEC-015 removes service-role client construction in five named Edge Function paths.

Each decision provides at least three credible options including a more aggressive collapse and a more permissive preservation — e.g., DEC-001 considers full collapse to one `person` vs separate per-spec tables vs the chosen middle, with a 'why not collapse' justification. DEC-009 explicitly considers QTI as the canonical content catalog as the aggressive alternative.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` declares projection_policy with DEC-016 and `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` is run in VERIFY.md. The seed contains 575 fields across 59 objects projecting to all five per-spec dictionaries, and PROGRESS confirms `--check passed with 5 projections, 59 objects, and 575 fields`.

The seed defines canonical objects (canonical.identity.person, canonical.context.class, canonical.outcome.line_item, etc.) with field-level projections listing per-spec dictionary refs. For example, canonical.identity.person.email projects to oneroster, caliper, integration-governance, and qti email fields.

`canonical.identity.person` exists as a top-level canonical object in the seed with fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind) and projections to OneRoster Person, Caliper Actor, QTI Candidate, LTI membership, and LTI launch subject_id. The QTI projection is documented as a `spec_only` exception with DEC-012 traceability.

Per-spec dictionaries (oneroster-core.v1.json, caliper-core.v1.json, case-core.v1.json, integration-governance-core.v1.json, qti-core.v1.json) carry `shared_enums` arrays with shared_enum_id, canonical_values, crosswalk, and reverse_crosswalk. Cross-spec values like `grading_progress` map both OneRoster `score_status` and LTI `grading_progress` to the same canonical values bidirectionally.

The seed declares 9 closed privacy classes (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) each citing DEC-019. All five per-spec dictionaries reproduce identical privacy_classes arrays. No `depends_on_*` placeholders appear.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` migration_generation block declares 11 runtime objects, 13 relationships, and `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py --check` runs in VERIFY.md. Generated `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` contains FK references derived from this graph, and PROGRESS confirms `1 migration, 11 tables, and 13 relationships` zero-diff.

Privacy classes, canonical fields, shared enums, and canonical values all carry `decision_id` references (DEC-019, DEC-018, DEC-001, DEC-007, etc.) that resolve to entries in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`.

`docs/spec-fidelity-report.md` is generated by `scripts/generate_spec_fidelity_report.py` (run in VERIFY.md with both `--report-only` and `--check`), reports 0 copy-exact seam defects, 0 extension fields missing rationale, and provides per-spec breakdown plus extension table with rationales for all 162 extension fields.

`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, `generate_caliper_core.py`, and `generate_integration_governance_core.py` produce HTML/Markdown/SQL/OpenAPI from per-spec dictionaries. VERIFY.md runs all five plus `build_site_docs.py`. The buildability guide links to dictionary anchors like `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.id`.

`docs/decision-map-for-builders.md` (rendered at `site/docs/decision-map-for-builders.html`, reachable from buildability guide and docs index) explains in plain language why person/class/enrollment/line_item/result/CASE-target/JWT/Caliper choices exist, linking each to DEC-001 through DEC-015. The buildability guide opens with a link to this map.

`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` walks through Step 0 bootstrap (psql + Auth Admin + token exchange), Step 1 create school+teacher+10 students, Step 2 create class+enrollments, Step 3 post numeric grade, Step 4 link assignment to CASE URI, Step 5 read Caliper-style class_activity_feed. Every step includes copy-pasteable curl/SQL with dictionary anchor links per field and enum value.

`site/api/site-link-check.json` reports 0 errors, 39 HTML pages, 330 local links, 634 dictionary entries, 20 decisions, and 12 API endpoints all reachable within ≤2 clicks (max_clicks=3). VERIFY.md runs `scripts/check_site_links.py` every iteration.

Simulated layperson reading only `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`, `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md`, and the linked decision map and dictionary anchors can produce executable curl/SQL for all five steps. Step 0 provides explicit 0a/0b/0c psql/curl/curl commands to bootstrap a fresh sandbox; Steps 1-5 give complete payloads with dictionary-linked field names and enum values. Self-hosted onboarding path is documented for users without platform admin access.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py` defines DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES per item and `normalize_traced_cause` ensures every non-pass verdict carries a non-empty traced_cause naming the lowest-layer artifact. `normalize_buildability_gaps` enforces traced_cause on every gap.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --runs 3 --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. Recent PROGRESS entries show fresh per-iteration reports with counts, traced_cause fields, and buildability_gaps arrays.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides`, `overrides_paused` checking literal PAUSE token outside HTML comments, calls `overrides_paused(overrides)` before each iteration, and `build_prompt` injects overrides under 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE — these supersede rubric defaults...)'.

`scripts/codex_loop.py._run_loop` reads `after_eval = load_verify_evaluator_output(...)` from the VERIFY-stage report and terminates only on `after_eval.get('done')`. When Codex stdout claims `LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE` but VERIFY says done=false, it logs 'Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.'

`maybe_publish` calls `git fetch origin main` then `git pull --rebase origin main` before `git push`. On rebase failure it captures conflicting paths via `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, runs `git rebase --abort`, and returns a `DIVERGENCE:` status that propagates to `_run_loop` which returns 1. PROGRESS records the divergence message.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py.deterministic_temperature_for_model` pins temperature=0 for non-opus-4-7 models. VERIFY runs `--runs 3`. `build_consensus_report` selects the safer lower-pass status using STATUS_SAFETY_RANK={fail:0, partial:1, blocked:2, pass:3} and forces done=false if any run reports done=false. Disagreements are persisted to the determinism block in `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` and to PROGRESS via `format_evaluator_runs_for_progress`.

`append_machine_progress` reads `after_eval` (loaded from VERIFY-stage `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` via `load_verify_evaluator_output` which enforces fresh mtime and ≥3 runs), formats VERIFY counts/done verbatim, and writes a `narrative_verify_mismatch` line when Codex stdout contains `LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE` while VERIFY done=false. Recent PROGRESS entries include all per-run statuses and disagreements.

`scripts/codex_loop.py.staged_eval_matches_verify` reads the staged `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` via `git show :site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, calls `comparable_eval` to strip `evaluatedAt`, compares against VERIFY output, and `maybe_publish` returns `skipped: staged evaluation does not match VERIFY output: <detail>` blocking commit on any other diff via `summarize_eval_diff`.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.

temperature.note: Anthropic Messages API does not expose a seed parameter. For models that still expose temperature, the evaluator pins it to 0. claude-opus-4-7 rejects the temperature parameter as deprecated, so the loop relies on N>=3 safer consensus for that model.

seed.note: Anthropic Messages API does not expose a seed parameter. For models that still expose temperature, the evaluator pins it to 0. claude-opus-4-7 rejects the temperature parameter as deprecated, so the loop relies on N>=3 safer consensus for that model.

determinism.consensus_rule: If all runs agree, keep the unanimous verdict. If any per-item status disagrees, select the safer lower-pass status using fail < partial < blocked < pass. If any run reports done=false, the consensus done flag is false.
